mapping.a_element: renormalization-group equations describing disorder as a marginally irrelevant perturbation at a fixed point
mapping.b_element: universal lattice artifacts vanishing as inverse powers of the logarithm of the correlation length in the Sine-Gordon model

mapping.a_element: logarithmic corrections to scaling due to quenched disorder in the 2D Ising model
mapping.b_element: logarithmic corrections (inverse powers of log correlation length) to continuum-limit quantities in the XY model

mapping.a_element: universality of logarithmic corrections arising from disorder
mapping.b_element: universality of leading lattice artifacts in the O(2) model

brief_justification: Both papers study universal logarithmic corrections to scaling behavior near a critical point, driven by marginally irrelevant perturbations (disorder in the Ising model, lattice artifacts in the XY model). The renormalization-group framework and the logarithmic form of corrections are the shared underlying machinery.
